titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 598

p.seg: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Know me to have lawfully received the church of Saint Cuthbert at Holy Island and the monks serving God there and their men and all their possessions under my firm peace and protection. I have also gratned to them that they be free and quit anywhere throughout my entire land from every toll on their demesne cattle. And I instruct that, wherever they can find fugitives of their land outside my demesne they may lawfully have them.

Wherefore I strictly forbid that anyone seek to cause injustice or violence to the said monks or their men, or demand toll from them for their demesne cattle, or unlawfully withhold from them fugitives of their land, on pain of my full forfeiture. As the charter of King David my grandfather, and the charter of King Malcolm my brother bears witness.
As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Archibald abbot of Dunfermline, William of Lindsay, Robert of London, my son, Gervase Avenel, David of Lindsay.
